name: A*B+C
description: Dual four-quadrant multiplier with VC offset. Each channel computes A·B+C · exponential attenuverters on B (-2…+2) and C (-1…+1) · OUT 1 normalizes to OUT 2 for chained four-channel mixing.

controls.name: B Level
controls.description: Attenuverter on the B input before multiplication. Exponential taper — gain change concentrated at the knob extremes.
controls.detail: ×-2 … 0 … ×+2 · noon = multiply by zero (mutes A·B term)

controls.name: C Level
controls.description: Attenuverter on the C input before summing with A·B. Exponential taper.

controls.name: B gain switch
controls.description: Three-position slide switch per B input, expanding the per-channel gain range beyond what the attenuverter alone allows.
controls.detail: three positions (up/centre/down) including unity, x2 and inverting stages — see panel silkscreen for V4 rev

controls.name: C gain switch
controls.description: Three-position slide switch per C input, extending the offset range.
controls.detail: three positions (up/centre/down) · combines with C Level exponential attenuverter

controls.name: Mode switch
controls.description: Two-position switch between multiplier mode and four-channel mixer mode. Mixer mode routes A1 into the OUT 2 sum, exposing four attenuated inputs to one master.
controls.detail: MULT: (A1·B1+C1) at OUT 1 · MIX: A1+A2 summed with B1,C1,B2,C2 attenuators into OUT 2

inputs.name: A 1
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 1, multiplied by B1. Audio or CV.

inputs.name: B 1
inputs.description: Second multiplicand for channel 1. Audio or CV. Multiplies A1 via the B1 attenuverter.
inputs.normalizedTo: +5V DC when unpatched, so B1 Level alone sets a static gain on A1

inputs.name: C 1
inputs.description: Offset / summing input for channel 1. Added to A1·B1 through the C1 attenuverter.
inputs.normalizedTo: +10V DC when unpatched, so C1 Level alone generates a bipolar offset (±10V)

inputs.name: A 2
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 2, multiplied by B2.

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Channel 1 result: A1·B1 + C1. Bicolour LED above the jack glows red for positive output, green for negative.

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Master output. When OUT 1 is unpatched, OUT 2 = (A1·B1+C1) + (A2·B2+C2). When OUT 1 is patched it leaves the sum, so OUT 2 carries channel 2 alone. Bicolour LED.
outputs.normalizedTo: channel 1 result summed into OUT 2 when OUT 1 is unpatched

behaviors.name: Four-quadrant multiplier
behaviors.trigger: audio into A and B of the same channel
behaviors.description: True four-quadrant multiplication: sign of A and sign of B both affect the output polarity. With bipolar audio on A and B the module produces classic ring-modulation sum/difference sidebands rather than a VCA's one-sided amplitude modulation.

behaviors.name: Exponential attenuverter response
behaviors.trigger: sweeping B Level or C Level
behaviors.description: Both attenuverters use exponential tapers, concentrating gain change near the full-CW and full-CCW ends. Small moves around noon have subtle effect; the last 20% of rotation produces the bulk of the amplitude change. Useful for fine-setting modulation depth then slamming it wide on demand.

behaviors.name: Normalised B = manual VCA
behaviors.trigger: B input unpatched, A patched, C unused (noon)
behaviors.description: B1 and B2 normalize to +5V DC. With nothing in B the attenuverter acts as a manual bipolar VCA on A: CCW inverts A, noon mutes, CW passes A at up to ×2 gain. No VC needed for static amplitude or polarity control.

behaviors.name: Normalised C = bipolar offset generator
behaviors.trigger: A and B unpatched, C unpatched, C Level in use
behaviors.description: C1 and C2 normalize to +10V DC. With nothing patched the channel outputs a clean ±10V manual offset scaled by C Level. Two independent bipolar DC sources per module — handy for tuning, thresholds or biasing CV signals.

behaviors.name: Output chaining for 4-channel mixing
behaviors.description: OUT 1 is internally normalized into the OUT 2 summing node. With OUT 1 empty, OUT 2 = channel 1 result + channel 2 result. Patch four signals into A1,C1,A2,C2 with all B inputs/attenuverters set appropriately and OUT 2 becomes a four-input mixer with attenuverters.

behaviors.name: Dual ring modulator
behaviors.trigger: audio on A1/B1 and A2/B2, both with B Level near full CW
behaviors.description: Each channel acts as an independent ring mod. Feed two pairs of audio sources and take OUT 1 and (with OUT 1 patched) OUT 2 for two independent metallic/clangorous timbres. With OUT 1 unpatched both ring-mod outputs sum at OUT 2.

behaviors.name: V4 per-input gain switches
behaviors.trigger: flipping SWB / SWC slide switches on V4 panel
behaviors.description: V4 adds a three-position slide switch per B and per C input extending gain range beyond the attenuverter alone. Positions include unity and doubled/inverted stages, widening headroom for cross-modulation and allowing more dramatic summing at OUT 2 without overdriving the multiplier stage.

behaviors.name: V4 mixer mode
behaviors.trigger: Mode switch flipped from MULT to MIX on V4
behaviors.description: V4 adds a global Mix switch. In MIX mode routing reconfigures so A1 joins A2 at the channel-2 sum, turning the module into a straightforward four-channel mixer (A1, A2, B1·C1-scaled, B2·C2-scaled) at OUT 2 with the multipliers acting as scalers rather than four-quadrant stages.

behaviors.name: LED polarity indicator
behaviors.description: Bicolour LEDs glow red on positive excursion, green on negative. At audio rate both illuminate equally — a solid orange glow means the output is symmetrically bipolar. A persistent red or green suggests a DC offset from C input or a unipolar A signal.

patchIdeas.name: Ring modulator pair
patchIdeas.patch: VCO 1 → A1 · VCO 2 → B1 · B1 Level full CW · C1 Level noon (no offset) · OUT 1 = four-quadrant product = classic ring-mod sum/difference tones · second voice on CH2 for parallel ring mod · detune the B VCOs for beating sidebands
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* B1 Level: full CW
* C1 Level: noon
* B2 Level: full CW
* C2 Level: noon

VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:

- VCO 1 (Out) -> ABC (A 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> ABC (B 1)
- ABC (OUT 1) -> Output (A) // ring mod 1
- VCO 3 (Out) -> ABC (A 2)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> ABC (B 2)
- ABC (OUT 2) -> Output (B) // ring mod 2 (patch OUT 1 to break sum)

patchIdeas.name: Dual VCA with bipolar depth
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → A · CV envelope → B · B Level sets depth, CCW side inverts the envelope action · C Level adjusts static offset (volume without CV) · exponential taper makes the last 20% of the knob the loud zone · OUT = VCA with voltage-controlled bipolar depth and manual bias
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* B1 Level: set depth
* C1 Level: static bias

VCO:
Envelope:

- VCO (Out) -> ABC (A 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> ABC (B 1)
- ABC (OUT 1) -> Output (In) // VCA with depth + bias

patchIdeas.name: Crossfader (CV-controlled)
patchIdeas.patch: Voice A → A1 · Voice B → A2 · bipolar LFO → B1 and (via attenuverter invert) B2 so one channel opens while the other closes · OUT 1 unpatched so OUT 2 sums both · smooth CV-controlled crossfade between two sources
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* B1 Level: full CW (non-inverted)
* B2 Level: full CCW (inverted)
* C1 & C2: noon

LFO:
Mult:
Voice A:
Voice B:

- Voice A (Out) -> ABC (A 1)
- Voice B (Out) -> ABC (A 2)
- LFO (Out) >> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) >> ABC (B 1) // opens CH1
- Mult (Out 2) >> ABC (B 2) // inverted = closes CH2
- ABC (OUT 2) -> Output (In) // crossfaded sum

patchIdeas.name: Four-input attenuverting mixer
patchIdeas.patch: Four CV sources → A1, C1, A2, C2 · B1 and B2 unpatched (norm +5V) so B·A = A times the B1/B2 Level setting · C1 and C2 Levels act as attenuverters on their inputs · OUT 1 unpatched · OUT 2 = sum of all four with independent bipolar scaling · instant modulation mixer with inversion
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* B1 & B2 Level: set A1/A2 scale (B unpatched)
* C1 & C2 Level: set C1/C2 attenuverter

Mod A:
Mod B:
Mod C:
Mod D:
Filter:

- Mod A (Out) >> ABC (A 1)
- Mod B (Out) >> ABC (C 1)
- Mod C (Out) >> ABC (A 2)
- Mod D (Out) >> ABC (C 2)
- ABC (OUT 2) >> Filter (Cutoff) // 4-input attenuverting sum

patchIdeas.name: Dual bipolar offset generator
patchIdeas.patch: Nothing in A, B or C on either channel · C normalized to +10V · C1 and C2 Level knobs become two independent ±10V manual offsets at OUT 1 and OUT 2 · patch OUT 1 to break the sum and get two discrete offsets · tune oscillators, bias filters, set S&H thresholds without a dedicated offset module
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* C1 Level: offset 1 amount
* C2 Level: offset 2 amount

VCO 1:
VCO 2:

- ABC (OUT 1) >> VCO 1 (V/Oct) // offset 1, ±10V
- ABC (OUT 2) >> VCO 2 (V/Oct) // offset 2 (OUT 1 patched breaks sum)

patchIdeas.name: Tremolo with voltage-controlled inversion
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → A1 · LFO → B1 · B1 Level at ~1 o'clock · C1 at noon · classic LFO tremolo · sweep B1 Level across noon manually or via SUM through another B2 path to flip tremolo polarity live · exponential taper keeps depth changes feeling musical at the extremes
patchIdeas.diagram:
ABC:
* B1 Level: ~1 o'clock (tremolo depth)
* C1 Level: noon

VCO:
LFO:

- VCO (Out) -> ABC (A 1)
- LFO (Out) >> ABC (B 1)
- ABC (OUT 1) -> Output (In) // tremolo
